summary.shape_sentence: Early push to a post-open high failed, then the session turned into a trend-down / sell-the-bounce day with a late panic flush and small rebound into the close.

goat.note: Late-session flush into the day's low near 26,545 produced the standout expansion move before a sharp rebound.

red_circles.behavior: Mostly marked failed bounce highs and bearish rejections after the opening peak, reinforcing lower-high structure through the day.

green_circles.behavior: Mostly appeared on oversold bounces from demand pockets; several worked tactically but were largely countertrend until the final rebound.

orange_banks_supply.behavior: Supply zones around 26,860-26,890 early and then 26,760-26,800 / 26,730-26,750 later were mostly respected and capped rebounds.

blue_banks_demand.behavior: Demand zones around 26,710-26,730, then 26,640-26,670 and near 26,600 were repeatedly tested; several broke before the final late-session bounce held the lower edge.

time_markers.10am_blue: Opening strength was peaking around this marker; price was near the morning high and close to rolling over.
time_markers.12pm_red: By noon the market was bearish, trading below dominant supply with rebound attempts failing under the red cloud.
time_markers.2pm_green: Around 14:00 price was stabilizing after midday weakness but still rotating below resistance rather than trending up.
time_markers.4pm_yellow: The session ended after a late downside flush and rebound; close was off the lows but still decisively red.

takeaway: Bearish RTH trend day that began with an opening push failure and spent the rest of the session selling rallies beneath descending supply. Useful as a reference for sessions where early strength exhausts fast, lunch stays weak, and the real expansion comes in a late downside flush before only a partial recovery.
